summary: Small config-only matching fix for `melee/gm/gmmain.c`: adjusted the GALE01 split boundary for `gmmain` `.data` and corrected two local string symbol ordinals so objdiff treats `gmmain` as matching. No source code was changed, and there were no PR comments or review notes in the provided slice.

what_changed.details: Changed `melee/gm/gmmain.c` `.data` split end from `0x803D4C38` to `0x803D4C32`, shrinking the assigned `.data` range by 6 bytes.
Renumbered the local `.data` string at `0x803D4BCC` from `@251` to `@256` while preserving address, size `0x18`, scope, and string metadata.
Renumbered the local `.sdata` string at `0x804D3EE8` from `@246` to `@251` while preserving address, size `0x3`, scope, and string metadata.

smart_moves: Fixed objdiff matching through metadata/config updates instead of touching C code, indicating the mismatch was caused by split/symbol attribution rather than implementation logic.
Adjusted only the relevant `gmmain` `.data` range and local autogenerated symbol names, keeping addresses and object sizes unchanged.
Used local string ordinal renumbering to align symbol identity with the compiler/decompilation output without altering the underlying data layout.
decomp_lessons: Objdiff mismatches can come from incorrect split boundaries or autogenerated local symbol numbering even when source code and data bytes are otherwise correct.
When a file appears non-matching in objdiff, verify `config/GALE01/splits.txt` section ranges before attempting source-level changes.
Local labels such as `@251`/`@256` are meaningful to matching tooling; preserving address and size is not always sufficient if the symbol name/ordinal differs from expected output.
A nearby global boundary label such as `lbl_803D4C38` may not imply the preceding compilation unit's split should extend exactly to that address; the split can end earlier.
naming_conventions: Autogenerated local string symbols use `@NNN` ordinal names in `symbols.txt`.
Global data symbols in this area use module-prefixed names such as `gmMainLib_803D4ABC`, `gmMainLib_804D3EE0`, and `gmMainLib_804D3EE4`.
Unidentified data boundary labels use `lbl_<address>` form, as seen with `lbl_803D4C38` and `lbl_804D3EF8`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: For objdiff config mismatches, compare split section endpoints against the actual emitted object boundaries and adjust `splits.txt` rather than forcing source changes.
If local string labels are out of order or assigned to the wrong section, renumber only the affected `@NNN` entries while keeping their addresses, sizes, and metadata intact.
Check both `.data` and `.sdata` symbol ordinals for the same translation unit; this PR fixed one local string in each section to make `gmmain` match.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that adjust GALE01 split boundaries to make objdiff match.
Find PRs that renumber local `@NNN` string symbols in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`.
Investigate whether `gmmain` has other section boundary labels near `lbl_803D4C38` that could affect matching.
